reasoning: Based on comprehensive analysis of inflation data through September 2025, the probability that US CPI will reach 4.1% or higher in any month of 2025 is very low. Current year-over-year inflation in September 2025 is 3.0%, requiring a 1.1 percentage point increase in just 2-3 months. Historical volatility of monthly inflation readings and current economic conditions make such a rapid acceleration extremely unlikely. Tariff effects have contributed only 0.5 percentage points to PCE inflation over a 3-month period (search_3_result_0), which is insufficient to bridge the gap to 4.1% given where we start from. Core inflation remains well-anchored around 3% (search_2_result_1, search_3_result_10). With limited time remaining in 2025 and no major catalysts for such dramatic inflation acceleration visible, probability is set at 14%.
